34	
35	You take **one high-effort blueprint declaration** and split it into a `\uses{}`-linked chain of **smaller, easier sub-lemmas**, so the prover formalizes a sequence of small goals instead of one large one. The target's proof becomes "by `L1`, `L2`, …, `L_n`", each `L_i` is a new block with its own statement, informal proof, and `\uses{}`, and each `L_i` is strictly easier than the original.
36	
37	You decompose DOWN (one node → many smaller ones). This is the complement of the dag-walker (which fills the cone UP). You do not invent new mathematics — you re-express the target's existing proof as a chain of named intermediate results.

72	   - a `\label{lem:<descriptive>}` and a `\lean{...}` hint (the Lean name the prover will use; name it by convention if the directive doesn't give one, and note it for the plan agent),

1	# Analogy: composite-adjunction unit cocycle without dependent-δ-splice
2	
3	## Mode
4	cross-domain-inspiration
5	
6	## Slug
7	d3cocycle006
8	
9	## Iteration
10	006
11	
12	## Structural problem (abstracted)
13	Prove an associativity/coherence (cocycle) identity for the unit — and the
14	oplax-monoidal comparison δ — of a composite of LEFT ADJOINTS stacked across
15	three categories (X→Y→Z), where each "leg" is itself a COMPOSITE adjunction
16	`(pullbackPushforward φ').comp sheafAdj`. The naive proof evaluates the mate /
17	conjugate at a component `.app P` and then tries to splice a local δ-square (or a
18	recovered `sheafCompPb·.hom` factor) into a long dependent composite by
19	`congrArg`/`conv`/`reassoc_of% key`. The splice never lands because the component
20	composite carries dependent indices — `eqToHom` of base-map equalities,
21	`Over.map`/`opensFunctor` reindex — so the rewrite motive is ill-typed.
22	
23	## Failed approaches (from directive)
24	- Direct `rw`/`erw` of the δ-square into the component composite: dependent indices block it.
25	- `congrArg`/`conv` to the δ-subterm: ill-typed motive under the dependent composite.
26	- Re-prove `sheafificationCompPullback_comp` wholesale via surjective/injective reduction
27	  of `leftAdjointCompNatTrans_assoc`: reduction opens but the final step is the same δ-splice.
28	
29	## Core finding (the technique that AVOIDS the splice)
30	
31	The dependent indices (`eqToHom`, `Over.map`/`opensFunctor` reindex) exist ONLY at
32	the **component** (`.app P`) level, AFTER the conjugate/mate has been evaluated into
33	a concrete composite of base-changed module objects. Mathlib's
34	`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Adjunction.Mates` proves EVERY composite-adjunction
35	coherence at the level of **natural transformations** — objects are functors, not
36	the dependent base-changed modules — where composition is plain whisker/`≫` and no
37	`eqToHom`/reindex term can appear. The whole identity is rewritten at the
38	transformation level and `.app P` is evaluated exactly ONCE, at the very end. There
39	is never a "splice a component δ-square into a dependent composite" step.
40	
41	The mate cocycle calculus (all in `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Adjunction/Mates.lean`):
42	
43	- `conjugateEquiv_comp` (Mates.lean): `conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α ≫ conjugateEquiv adj₂ adj₃ β
44	  = conjugateEquiv adj₁ adj₃ (β ≫ α)`. **The abstract δ-/factor-splice**, as a NatTrans
45	  equation. Combining two legs' conjugates into the composite's conjugate is one rewrite
46	  with NO dependent indices.
47	- `iterated_mateEquiv_conjugateEquiv` (Mates.lean): the iterated mate of a TwoSquare across
48	  two stacked adjunctions = the conjugate of the COMPOSITE adjunction `adj₁.comp adj₄`. **This
49	  IS the composite-adjunction coherence**, stated as a whole-transformation equality.
50	- `mateEquiv_vcomp` / `mateEquiv_hcomp` / `mateEquiv_square` (Mates.lean): the bicategorical
51	  functoriality of mates (vertical/horizontal/4-square paste) — each ONE transformation
52	  equation, the analogue of the project's "4 interleaved δ-squares".
53	- `unit_conjugateEquiv` (Mates.lean): unit-naturality of the mate, `adj₁.unit ≫ (conj α).app(L₁ ·)
54	  = adj₂.unit ≫ R₂.map (α ·)`. **Already used by the project** (`sheaf_unit_comp_pushforward_pullbackComp_inv`, L2422).
55	- `conjugateEquiv_mateEquiv_vcomp` / `mateEquiv_conjugateEquiv_vcomp`: mixed whisker-composition
56	  of a conjugate with a mate — the abstract form of the project's `hwr := conjugateEquiv_whiskerRight`
57	  device (L2598).
58	
59	Mathlib's PROOF technique for these: `ext` once to a component, then `simp` with the
60	explicit whiskered `mateEquiv_apply` normal form + the unit/counit triangle identities
61	(`Adjunction.left_triangle_components` etc.). The cocycle lemmas are CONSUMED by rewriting
62	at the transformation level, NOT by splicing into a component.
63	
64	## Analogues found
65	
66	### Analogue: `CategoryTheory.conjugateEquiv_comp` (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Adjunction/Mates.lean`)
67	- **Domain**: category theory / adjunction-mate calculus (one shelf over from sheaves-of-modules).
68	- **Same problem there**: combine the conjugates (mates) of two composable left-adjoint NatTrans
69	  into the conjugate of their composite — exactly the "glue the f-leg and h-leg factors into the
70	  (h≫f) factor" step, but at the transformation level.
71	- **Technique**: a single equation between right-adjoint NatTrans. Contravariant composition
72	  `conj β ≫ conj α = conj (β ≫ α)`. No component, no `eqToHom`.
73	- **Mapping to project**: the project recovers `R1 = (pullback h).map (sheafCompPb f).hom.app P`
74	  and `R5 = (sheafCompPb h).hom.app (PrPb_f P)` and wants to fuse them into the `(h≫f)` unit. Via
75	  `sheafificationCompPullback_eq_leftAdjointUniq` (L1534) each `sheafCompPb·.hom` is
76	  `(leftAdjointUniq A· B·).hom`, whose `homEquiv`-transpose is `conjugateEquiv B· A· (…)` /
77	  `B·.unit` (`homEquiv_leftAdjointUniq_hom_app`, used L1586/1626/2726). So R1≫R5 fuse by
78	  `conjugateEquiv_comp` into the single `(h≫f)`-conjugate = `B_{h≫f}.unit` — replacing the failed
79	  `reassoc_of% key` splice (L2786) and the un-consumable `hwr` component device (L2598).
80	- **Porting cost**: low. The model lemma `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` (L920–993) is the
81	  bare-adjunction instance of this exact assembly (it uses `homEquiv_naturality_left/right` +
82	  `unit_conjugateEquiv` + `pushforwardComp.hom.naturality`, NO component splice). The sheafification
83	  version differs only in that B_f/B_h are COMPOSITE adjunctions, so the single bare
84	  `pushforwardComp.hom.naturality` slide (model L989) becomes a `conjugateEquiv_comp` fuse.
85	- **Verdict**: ANALOGUE_FOUND.
86	
87	### Analogue: `CategoryTheory.iterated_mateEquiv_conjugateEquiv` (`Mates.lean`)
88	- **Domain**: category theory / mate calculus.
89	- **Same problem there**: the mate of a square taken across TWO stacked adjunctions equals the
90	  conjugate of the single COMPOSITE adjunction — the composite-adjunction coherence itself.
91	- **Technique**: whole-transformation equality; the stacking `adj₁.comp adj₄` is handled once,
92	  abstractly, with no per-component reindex.
93	- **Mapping to project**: this is the abstract statement of "B_{h≫f}.unit decomposes through B_f, B_h
94	  + the pseudofunctor coherences" that `sheafificationCompPullback_comp_tail` is trying to prove by
95	  hand. Frame the tail as `conjugateEquiv` of the composite `sheafAdj.comp (PrPbPush (h≫f))` and let
96	  this lemma + `conjugateEquiv_comp` discharge the stacking, instead of the manual `hinner`/`hcomp'`
97	  twin + splice.
98	- **Porting cost**: medium. Needs the project's three adjunctions cast as the `adj₁..adj₄` stack and
99	  the TwoSquare identified; heavier than just `conjugateEquiv_comp`, but it is the principled frame.
100	- **Verdict**: ANALOGUE_FOUND.
101	
102	### Analogue: `CategoryTheory.Adjunction.isMonoidal_comp` + `Functor.OplaxMonoidal.comp_δ` (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Monoidal/Functor.lean`)
103	- **Domain**: monoidal category theory (the δ / tensorator side).
104	- **Same problem there**: the oplax-monoidal structure (δ) of a composite of left adjoints is the
105	  composite oplax structure; `comp_δ` expresses δ of a composite functor via the two legs' δ — a
106	  TRANSFORMATION identity, not a component splice.
107	- **Technique**: `isMonoidal_comp` supplies `(adj.comp adj').IsMonoidal` so the composite's δ is
108	  definitionally the legs' δ glued; `comp_δ` is the rewrite. No dependent component appears until
109	  evaluation.
110	- **Mapping to project**: `pullbackTensorMap_restrict` (D3′, L2824) splices the δ-square of
111	  `pullback φ'_{h≫f}` into the dependent 4-fold composite. Instead: identify `pullback φ'_{h≫f}`'s
112	  oplax δ with the composite via `isMonoidal_comp` + `comp_δ` + the already-CLOSED project lemma
113	  `pullbackComp_δ` (L2282), at the transformation level, then conjugate the WHOLE 4-fold composite
114	  once via the `(h≫f)`-adjunction `homEquiv` (mirroring `mateEquiv_square`, the 4-square paste as one
115	  equation) before evaluating `.app`. Avoids the in-place δ-square `rw`.
116	- **Porting cost**: medium. Needs `OplaxMonoidal`/`IsMonoidal` instances on the project's pullback
117	  adjunctions plumbed through (the D1′ `show … from`/`let φ' : … ⋙ forget₂` monoidal-pinning device,
118	  noted L2888–2895); `pullbackComp_δ` is already done.
119	- **Verdict**: ANALOGUE_FOUND.

121	## Top suggestion
122	
123	Start with `sheafificationCompPullback_comp_tail` (L2467) using `conjugateEquiv_comp` (Mates.lean).
124	The model `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` (L920–993) is a working, axiom-clean template of the SAME

126	`homEquiv_naturality_left/right`, `unit_conjugateEquiv`, and `pushforwardComp.hom.naturality`. Port it

30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. 34	```

36	  --name <subagent-name> \

42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.

44	**A dispatch is a BLOCKING Bash call — wait by staying inside it.** `archon-subagent.py` is synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and returns only once the child finishes and its report is on disk). A long dispatch may be auto-backgrounded with a task ID — that's fine; wait for that task's result before acting. **Do NOT use the `Monitor` tool to wait** — it is non-blocking (returns immediately, "keep working"), so your turn ends and the subagents are abandoned mid-run. **Do NOT use foreground `sleep` / `until … ; do sleep … ; done`** — the harness blocks `sleep`. 75

95	
96	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
97	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.

100	
101	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake build <module>` (it uses `lake build`, not `lake env lean <file>`, so dependencies are built and missing-import failures aren't spurious), and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. The one exception is the review agent: if it is **certain** the sync got a marker wrong (a clearly-finished proof left unmarked, or a marker present on something incomplete), it may manually correct it, but must explicitly surface and justify that override in its summary (see `.archon/prompts/review.md`).
102	
103	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.

106

108	
109	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
110	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
111	
112	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
113	
114	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
115	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true or important at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
116	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
117	
118	## Agent Roles
119	
120	### Plan Agent
121	
122	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
123	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
124	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
125	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
126	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
127	- Curate `ARCHON_MEMORY.md` — the condensed, cross-iteration knowledge file injected (read-only) into every agent's prompt. Keep only durable facts that would surprise an agent reading the code fresh (dead-end tactics, files not to touch, Mathlib gap coordinates, protected invariants). Hard limits: ~10 bullets / ~600 chars; prune before adding. Do NOT duplicate what's obvious from the code or PROGRESS.md.
128	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
129	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
130	
131	### Prover Agent
132	
133	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
134	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
135	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
136	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
137	- A prover lane ends by writing its task result and exiting normally. **Do not kill Lean, Lake, shell, or sibling agent processes from inside the prover lane.** Do not run `pkill`, `killall`, regex-based process cleanup, or background-verification cleanup. If a verification command is still running, wait for its result or report that it is still running; process lifetime is owned by the Archon runner/orchestrator.
138	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
139	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
140	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
141	
142	### Subagents
143	
144	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
145	
146	### Review Agent
147	
148	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
149	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
150	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
151	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
152	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Normally leave `\leanok` to the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase; only override a marker the sync got demonstrably wrong, and justify it in your summary.
153	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
154	
155	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
156	
157	These run automatically between phases:
158	
159	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
160	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
161	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.

\begin{lemma}
[Sheafification kills the \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\) hom--inv pair (D3$'$ residual brick)]
  \label{lem:sheafify_pullbackcomp_hom_inv_cancel}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.sheafifyMap_pullbackComp_hom_inv_id}
  Let \(h : Z \to Y\) and \(f : Y \to X\) be composable, write
  \(\mathtt{PrPbComp} = \mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp}\,\varphi'_f\,\varphi'_h\) for the
  presheaf pullback pseudofunctoriality isomorphism
  \(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi'_f \circ \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi'_h \cong
  \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi'_{h\circ f}\), and let \(a_Z\) be sheafification of presheaves of
  modules on \(Z\). Then for every presheaf \(T\) over \(X\) the sheafified hom--inv pair cancels:
  \[
    a_Z.\mathrm{map}\bigl(\mathtt{PrPbComp}.\mathrm{hom}.\mathrm{app}\,T\bigr) \;\mathbin{;}\;
    a_Z.\mathrm{map}\bigl(\mathtt{PrPbComp}.\mathrm{inv}.\mathrm{app}\,T\bigr) \;=\;
    \mathrm{id}_{a_Z.\mathrm{map}\,(\cdots)}.
  \]
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  The two factors are \(a_Z.\mathrm{map}\) of the components at \(T\) of the hom--inv pair of the
  isomorphism \(\mathtt{PrPbComp}\). By \(\mathtt{Iso.hom\_inv\_id\_app}\) the underlying composite
  \(\mathtt{PrPbComp}.\mathrm{hom}.\mathrm{app}\,T \mathbin{;} \mathtt{PrPbComp}.\mathrm{inv}.\mathrm{app}\,T\)
  is the identity; applying \(a_Z.\mathrm{map}\) (\(\mathtt{congrArg}\), then \(\mathtt{Functor.map\_comp}\)
  to split and \(\mathtt{Functor.map\_id}\) to collapse the identity) yields the claim. The
  cancellation is built as this \emph{standalone} equation and then spliced into the D3\(^\prime\)
  paste by \(\mathtt{erw}\); a plain \(\mathtt{rw}\) does not fire across the parenthesised group
  boundary because of the \(\mathtt{Sheaf.val}\,Z\) carrier-spelling mismatch.
\end{proof}
